top_stories.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
top_stories.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about Kali365, a phishing-as-a-service platform targeting Microsoft 365 accounts. The platform abuses OAuth device code authentication flows to steal session tokens and bypass MFA protections.

top_stories.title: Announcing The Forrester Wave™: Workforce Identity Platforms, Q2 2026
top_stories.summary: Forrester has released its Q2 2026 Wave evaluation of workforce identity security platforms, assessing and ranking major providers in the space. The report reflects the growing strategic importance of workforce identity security in modern cybersecurity frameworks.

top_stories.title: AI Agents Are Shifting Identity Security Budget Dynamics
top_stories.summary: New Omdia research examines how AI agent identity management is creating distinct budget dynamics compared to traditional IAM projects. The findings address the growing need to manage, secure, and govern AI agent identities as these projects proliferate across enterprises.

top_stories.title: Manage machine identities: The hidden privileged access layer you need to manage
top_stories.summary: Delinea published guidance on managing machine identities (non-human identities), covering service accounts, service principals, workload roles, OAuth apps, AI agents, and IAM roles. The article addresses how these identities authenticate using credentials such as access keys, secrets, and tokens, and highlights the privileged access risks they present.

top_stories.title: Lessons for organizations from the Verizon 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report
top_stories.summary: This article analyzes lessons from the Verizon 2026 Data Breach Investigations Report (DBIR), an annual report that consistently highlights credential theft, authentication weaknesses, and access-related attack vectors as leading causes of breaches. The DBIR is a widely referenced source for IAM professionals given its detailed breakdown of how identity-related factors contribute to data breaches.

news.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign called TrapDoor has distributed credential-stealing malware across 34+ malicious packages and 384+ versions on npm, PyPI, and Crates.io. The campaign began May 22, 2026, with packages published in coordinated waves across the three package ecosystems.

news.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
news.summary: GitHub has introduced staged publishing for npm, requiring maintainers to complete a 2FA challenge before packages become publicly available. The feature adds human-gated approval controls to npm package releases as a supply chain security measure.

news.title: Working with Claims Mapping Policies in Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: The article covers how to customize token claims in Microsoft Entra ID using Claims Mapping Policies for OAuth, OpenID Connect, and SAML tokens. It details the process of adding user attributes such as first and last name to tokens and assigning these policies to Service Principals.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach now under congressional scrutiny. CISA is working to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials while lawmakers from both chambers demand answers.

news.title: Security hardening to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health - Microsoft Entra ID
news.summary: Microsoft is implementing security hardening changes to the autoupgrade process for Microsoft Entra Connect and Microsoft Entra Connect Health. The update affects the automated upgrade mechanism for these hybrid identity synchronization tools used to connect on-premises Active Directory environments with Microsoft Entra ID.

news.title: Microsoft recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms
news.summary: Microsoft has been recognized as a Leader in The Forrester Wave™ for Workforce Identity Security Platforms, Q2 2026. The company received the highest scores in both the current offering and strategy categories in the evaluation.

news.title: Identity security for AI agents: The proliferation challenge
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging challenge of securing AI agent identities, distinguishing them from human identities and traditional non-human identities (NHIs). It focuses on how identity teams must adapt their security foundations to manage the nondeterministic behavior of AI agents as adoption accelerates.

news.title: Most data breaches start with a stolen password. Here’s how to fix that
news.summary: The article addresses the common problem of weak password practices in organizations, including credential reuse and insecure sharing. It focuses on general password hygiene and credential management recommendations rather than specific IAM products or enterprise-level solutions.

news.title: GCC firms rethink cyber defences as AI phishing surges
news.summary: Security leaders in Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) firms are accelerating investments in identity security and rethinking trust models in response to AI-powered phishing campaigns expanding beyond email into platforms like Teams, calendars, and AI tools. The shift reflects growing adoption of zero trust frameworks as attack surfaces broaden across collaboration and productivity tools.

news.title: OktaがClaude Compliance APIと統合、Claude環境内のアイデンティティ保護を強化
news.summary: Okta has announced integration with Anthropic's Claude Compliance API to strengthen identity protection within Claude environments. The integration involves Okta's Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM) product to extend identity security capabilities to Claude AI environments.

news.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
news.summary: Microsoft has disclosed two actively exploited vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, including a privilege escalation flaw (CVE-2026-41091, CVSS 7.8) that allows attackers to gain SYSTEM privileges via improper link resolution. The second vulnerability is a denial-of-service flaw, both confirmed as under active exploitation in the wild.

news.title: When Identity is the Attack Path
news.summary: An analysis examines how a single cached AWS access key on a Windows machine—stored through standard credential caching behavior rather than misconfiguration—could expose up to 98% of a company's cloud entities to attackers. The piece illustrates how routine identity and credential storage mechanisms create significant attack paths without any policy violations occurring.

news.title: Why Okta (OKTA) Sees AI Agents as an Identity-Security Opportunity
news.summary: Okta is positioning AI agents as an opportunity within its identity security business. The article frames this in the context of OKTA stock performance and a potential turnaround investment thesis.

news.title: WSO2 Accelerates Agentic Enterprise Adoption with New Agent Identity, Forward Deployed Engineers, and Expanded Delivery Partner Ecosystem
news.summary: WSO2 has expanded its Agent Fabric platform with new agent identity capabilities targeting AI-native enterprise deployments. The update includes identity management features for agentic AI systems alongside forward deployed engineering services and an expanded delivery partner ecosystem.

news.title: Semperis Takes Center Stage at Infosec Europe 2026
news.summary: Semperis, an identity-driven cyber resilience vendor, is participating in Infosec Europe 2026 with a featured panel discussion. The panel includes industry figures discussing a project called "Midnight in the War Room," with no specific product launches or major announcements detailed.

news.title: Protecting collaboration with behavior-based access controls
news.summary: The article discusses behavior-based access controls as a method for securing modern collaboration sessions where traditional security tools fall short. The piece focuses on dynamic access control mechanisms that adapt based on user behavior patterns rather than static permissions.

news.title: Implement SAML SSO Authentication in Laravel Filament with Socialite
news.summary: This article provides a developer tutorial on implementing SAML SSO authentication in Laravel Filament using the Socialite library and SAML2 provider. The guide covers authentication flow components including metadata endpoints, redirects, and callbacks for enterprise applications.

news.title: Agent AI is Coming. Are You Ready?
news.summary: Orchid Security released its "Identity Gap: Snapshot 2026" report on May 19, 2026, examining unmanaged identity elements in enterprise environments. The report found that "identity dark matter" — unseen and unmanaged identity components — now accounts for 57% of enterprise identity versus 43% for visible, managed identity, coinciding with increased enterprise adoption of AI agents.

news.title: CISA Exposes Secrets, Credentials in 'Private' Repo
news.summary: CISA inadvertently exposed secrets and credentials in a GitHub repository named "Private-CISA" that was publicly accessible since November 2025. The leaked repository contained sensitive credentials, highlighting a secrets management and access control failure at a major cybersecurity agency.

news.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
news.summary: A set of vulnerabilities dubbed "Claw Chain" were discovered in the OpenClaw AI agent framework, now patched, that allowed attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence. The flaws affected deployments of the rapidly growing framework, with the attack chain targeting authentication credentials and privilege escalation paths.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, constituting a significant privileged credential exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft for his role in phishing attacks targeting major technology companies in 2022. The attacks used SMS phishing to compromise at least a dozen companies and steal tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency, with Scattered Spider known for social engineering and MFA bypass techniques used in high-profile breaches.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from users across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain access to targeted accounts.
